156	/-- **The sections functor `Γ(V,-)` is corepresented by `jShriekOU V`.** A natural isomorphism
157	`sectionsFunctor V ≅ preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op (jShriekOU V))`, assembled from the additive
158	corepresentability bijection `jShriekOU_homEquiv` and its naturality. Project-local: the functorial
159	upgrade of `jShriekOU_homEquiv`, the geometric input to the absolute-cohomology bridge. -/
160	noncomputable def sectionsFunctorCorepIso (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
161	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor V ≅ preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op (jShriekOU V)) :=
162	  NatIso.ofComponents
163	    (fun F => (AddEquiv.toAddCommGrpIso (jShriekOU_homEquiv V F)).symm)
164	    (by
165	      intro F₁ F₂ g
166	      ext s
167	      simp only [AddEquiv.toAddCommGrpIso, comp_apply, Iso.symm_hom, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom,
168	        AddEquiv.coe_toAddMonoidHom]
169	      rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_eq]
170	      change (ConcreteCategory.hom ((AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor V).map g)) s
171	        = jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₂ ((jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₁).symm s ≫ g)
172	      rw [jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat V ((jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₁).symm s) g, AddEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
173	      rfl)

175	/-- **Right derived functors transport along a natural isomorphism of additive functors.**
176	Project-local: assembled from `NatTrans.rightDerived` of `τ.hom`/`τ.inv` and functoriality
177	(`NatTrans.rightDerived_comp`, `NatTrans.rightDerived_id`). -/
178	noncomputable def rightDerivedNatIso {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D] [Abelian C]
179	    [HasInjectiveResolutions C] [Abelian D] {F G : C ⥤ D} [F.Additive] [G.Additive]
180	    (τ : F ≅ G) (n : ℕ) : F.rightDerived n ≅ G.rightDerived n where
181	  hom := NatTrans.rightDerived τ.hom n
182	  inv := NatTrans.rightDerived τ.inv n
183	  hom_inv_id := by rw [← NatTrans.rightDerived_comp, τ.hom_inv_id, NatTrans.rightDerived_id]
184	  inv_hom_id := by rw [← NatTrans.rightDerived_comp, τ.inv_hom_id, NatTrans.rightDerived_id]

186	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Ext` transport along the spectrum equivalence (Need #1)
187	
188	The whole-scheme spectrum isomorphism `U ≅ Spec Γ(U,𝒪)` (`Scheme.isoSpec`, a genuine iso because
189	`U` is affine) induces an *equivalence* of module categories `U.Modules ≌ (Spec Γ U).Modules`,
190	assembled from `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` along the iso and its coherences. An equivalence functor
191	is fully faithful, exact, and preserves injective objects, so
192	`Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects` upgrades `Ext.mapExactFunctor` along it
193	to an *isomorphism* of `Ext`-groups. This transports the residual general-affine-open Serre
194	vanishing for `U` to the `Spec`-developed `affine_serre_vanishing_general_open`.
195	
196	The pushforward-of-an-iso construction is generic in the scheme isomorphism `φ : X ≅ Y`. -/
197	
198	/-- **A scheme isomorphism induces an equivalence of module categories.** For `φ : X ≅ Y`, the
199	pushforward `Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom` is an equivalence with quasi-inverse the pushforward
200	along `φ.inv`; the unit/counit are supplied by the
201	`pushforwardId`/`pushforwardComp`/`pushforwardCongr` coherences. Project-local: Mathlib packages the
202	pushforward functor and its coherences but not the equivalence assembled from an iso (the genuine
203	equivalence used to transport `Ext` for Need #1). -/
204	noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) :
205	    X.Modules ≌ Y.Modules :=
206	  CategoryTheory.Equivalence.mk
207	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom) (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.inv)
208	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId X).symm ≪≫ Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr φ.hom_inv_id.symm ≪≫
209	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp φ.hom φ.inv).symm)
210	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp φ.inv φ.hom ≪≫ Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr φ.inv_hom_id ≪≫
211	      Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y)
212	
213	/-- The functor of `pushforwardEquivOfIso φ` is (defeq) the additive `pushforward φ.hom`. -/
214	noncomputable instance pushforwardEquivOfIso_functor_additive {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) :
215	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.Additive :=
216	  inferInstanceAs (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom).Additive
217	
218	/-- **`Ext` transport along a scheme isomorphism.** For `φ : X ≅ Y` and `A B : X.Modules`, the
219	equivalence `pushforwardEquivOfIso φ` (whose functor is fully faithful, exact, and injective-object
220	preserving) upgrades the additive map `Ext.mapExactFunctor` to an isomorphism of `Ext`-groups
221	`Ext A B n ≃+ Ext (φ_* A) (φ_* B) n`, via `Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects`.
222	Project-local: the `Ext`-isomorphism half of Need #1 (the spectrum-equivalence transport). -/
223	noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y)
224	    [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (A B : X.Modules) (n : ℕ) :
225	    CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext A B n ≃+
226	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj A)
227	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj B) n :=
228	  AddEquiv.ofBijective ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.mapExtAddHom A B n)
229	    (Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects
230	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor A B n)
231	
232	/-- **Need #1 — `Ext` transport along the whole-scheme spectrum equivalence.** For an affine scheme
233	`U`, the canonical iso `U ≅ Spec Γ(U,𝒪)` (`Scheme.isoSpec`) induces, for every `q`, an isomorphism
234	`Ext^q_U(A, B) ≅ Ext^q_{Spec Γ U}(φ_* A, φ_* B)`. In particular the residual `Ext` over `U` vanishes
235	iff its transport over `Spec Γ U` does. Project-local target of `lem:modules_isoSpec_ext_transport`.
236	
237	The remaining identification of the transported arguments — `φ_*(jShriekOU V) ≅ jShriekOU V'` for
238	`V'` the (affine) image open, and quasi-coherence of `φ_* H` — is handed off (see the task-result
239	file); it is orthogonal to this transport and is the geometric naturality of `j_!` under a scheme
240	iso. -/
241	noncomputable def modulesIsoSpecExtTransport (U : Scheme.{u}) [IsAffine U]
242	    [EnoughInjectives U.Modules] (A B : U.Modules) (n : ℕ) :
243	    CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext A B n ≃+
244	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext
245	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso U.isoSpec).functor.obj A)
246	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso U.isoSpec).functor.obj B) n :=
247	  Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv U.isoSpec A B n

249	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Ext`-vanishing ⟹ coyoneda right-derived vanishing
250	
251	The homological-algebra half of the relative-affine vanishing argument (Bridge (1)/(2) remainder).
252	The `q`-th right derived functor of the additive Hom-functor `Hom(P,-) = preadditiveCoyoneda(op P)`,
253	evaluated at `H`, is computed (via the chosen injective resolution `I•` of `H`,
254	`InjectiveResolution.isoRightDerivedObj`) as the homology at degree `q` of the cochain complex of
255	abelian groups `n ↦ (P ⟶ Iⁿ)`.  By `InjectiveResolution.extMk` / `extMk_eq_zero_iff`, that homology
256	*is* `Ext P H q`: a degree-`q` cocycle is a map `f : P ⟶ Iq` with `f ≫ d = 0`, and it is a coboundary
257	iff the class `extMk f` vanishes in `Ext`.  Hence if every class of `Ext P H q` vanishes (`q ≥ 1`),
258	the Hom-complex is exact at `q` and the right-derived object is a zero object.  Project-local: Mathlib's
259	`Ext` is built on the derived category, not on `Functor.rightDerived`, so this bridge is absent. -/
260	
261	/-- The differential of the Hom-cochain-complex `n ↦ (P ⟶ Iⁿ)` (the image of a cochain complex `K`
262	under `preadditiveCoyoneda(op P)`) acts by post-composition with the differential of `K`. -/
263	private lemma preadditiveCoyoneda_mapHomologicalComplex_d_apply {C : Type*} [Category C]
264	    [Preadditive C] (P : C) (K : CochainComplex C ℕ) (i j : ℕ) (g : P ⟶ K.X i) :
265	    (((preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op P)).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj K).d i j g
266	      = g ≫ K.d i j := by
267	  rw [Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_obj_d]
268	  rfl
269	
270	/-- **`Ext`-vanishing forces the coyoneda right-derived object to vanish.** If every class of
271	`Ext P H q` is zero (`q ≥ 1`), then `(Hom(P,-)).rightDerived q` is a zero object at `H`. The
272	homological-algebra bridge between the project's `Ext`-based Serre vanishing and the
273	`Functor.rightDerived` description of the higher-direct-image presheaf homology. Project-local. -/
274	lemma isZero_coyoneda_rightDerived_of_forall_ext_eq_zero {C : Type*} [Category C]
275	    [Abelian C] [HasInjectiveResolutions C] [CategoryTheory.HasExt C] (P H : C) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q)
276	    (hExt : ∀ e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext P H q, e = 0) :
277	    IsZero (((preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op P)).rightDerived q).obj H) := by
278	  obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero hq.ne'
279	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((injectiveResolution H).isoRightDerivedObj
280	    (preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op P)) (p + 1))
281	  set R := injectiveResolution H with hR
282	  refine HomologicalComplex.ExactAt.isZero_homology ?_
283	  rw [HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff' _ p (p + 1) (p + 2) (by simp) (by simp),
284	    ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff]
285	  intro x₂ hx₂
286	  have hf : x₂ ≫ R.cocomplex.d (p + 1) (p + 2) = 0 := by
287	    rw [← preadditiveCoyoneda_mapHomologicalComplex_d_apply P R.cocomplex (p + 1) (p + 2) x₂]
288	    exact hx₂
289	  obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := (R.extMk_eq_zero_iff x₂ (p + 2) rfl hf p rfl).mp (hExt _)
290	  refine ⟨g, ?_⟩
291	  rw [← hg]
292	  exact preadditiveCoyoneda_mapHomologicalComplex_d_apply P R.cocomplex p (p + 1) g
293	
294	/-- **`[HasInjectiveResolutions C]` provides enough injectives.** The degree-`0` term of the chosen
295	injective resolution, with its quasi-iso unit, is an injective presentation. Project-local connector
296	(Mathlib derives `HasInjectiveResolutions` from `EnoughInjectives` + `Abelian`, not conversely);
297	needed to feed `[EnoughInjectives U.Modules]` to the spectrum `Ext`-transport from
298	`[HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules]`. -/
299	lemma enoughInjectives_of_hasInjectiveResolutions {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C]
300	    [HasInjectiveResolutions C] : EnoughInjectives C where
301	  presentation X := by
302	    obtain ⟨R⟩ := (HasInjectiveResolutions.out X).out
303	    exact ⟨{ J := R.cocomplex.X 0, injective := R.injective 0, f := R.ι.f 0
304	             mono := CategoryTheory.InjectiveResolution.instMonoFNatι R 0 }⟩

306	/-- **An isomorphism in the first `Ext`-argument transfers subsingletons.** If `Ext B Y q` is a
307	subsingleton and `φ : A ≅ B`, then so is `Ext A Y q` (every class is the `φ.hom`-precomposite of its
308	`φ.inv`-precomposite, which vanishes). Project-local: the contravariant `Ext`-functoriality packaged
309	for the Serre-vanishing transport along the `jShriekOU` transport iso. -/
310	lemma subsingleton_ext_of_iso_fst {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C] [CategoryTheory.HasExt C]
311	    {A B : C} (φ : A ≅ B) (Y : C) (q : ℕ)
312	    [Subsingleton (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext B Y q)] :
313	    Subsingleton (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext A Y q) := by
314	  refine ⟨fun a b => ?_⟩
315	  suffices h : ∀ z : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext A Y q, z = 0 by rw [h a, h b]
316	  intro z
317	  have hw : (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ φ.inv).comp z (zero_add q) = 0 := Subsingleton.elim _ _
318	  have hz : z = (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ φ.hom).comp
319	      ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ φ.inv).comp z (zero_add q)) (zero_add q) := by
320	    rw [Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀_assoc, φ.hom_inv_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp]
321	  rw [hz, hw, Abelian.Ext.comp_zero]

323	/-- **Serre `Ext`-vanishing over an affine scheme via the spectrum transport.** Let `U` be a scheme,
324	`φ : U ≅ Spec R` a scheme isomorphism, `V ⊆ U` an open and `H` an `𝒪_U`-module.  If the pushforward
325	`Φ(j_!𝒪_V)` of the corepresenting object is isomorphic to `j_!𝒪_{V'}` for an *affine* open
326	`V' ⊆ Spec R`, and `Φ H` is quasi-coherent, then every class of `Ext^q_U(j_!𝒪_V, H)` vanishes for
327	`q ≥ 1`.  Assembled from `pushforwardExtAddEquiv` (the spectrum transport), the contravariant
328	`Ext`-transfer `subsingleton_ext_of_iso_fst`, and the now-unconditional
329	`affine_serre_vanishing_general_open` on `Spec R`.  Project-local: the Bridge (2) Serre-vanishing
330	leaf of the open-immersion acyclicity, parameterized over the geometric transport data so that the
331	residual `jShriekOU`/quasi-coherence transport sub-lemmas are isolated. -/
332	lemma ext_jShriekOU_eq_zero_of_specIso {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (U : Scheme.{u})
333	    [HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules] (φ : U ≅ Spec R) (V : U.Opens) (H : U.Modules)
334	    (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (V' : (Spec R).Opens) (hV' : IsAffineOpen V')
335	    (hjt : (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj (jShriekOU V) ≅ jShriekOU V')
336	    (hqc : ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj H).IsQuasicoherent)
337	    (e : Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V) H q) : e = 0 := by
338	  haveI : EnoughInjectives U.Modules := enoughInjectives_of_hasInjectiveResolutions
339	  set Φ := Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ with hΦ
340	  haveI : EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules := EnoughInjectives.of_equivalence Φ.inverse
341	  haveI : (Φ.functor.obj H).IsQuasicoherent := hqc
342	  haveI hsub0 : Subsingleton (Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V') (Φ.functor.obj H) q) :=
343	    ⟨fun a b => (affine_serre_vanishing_general_open _ V' hV' q hq a).trans
344	      (affine_serre_vanishing_general_open _ V' hV' q hq b).symm⟩
345	  haveI hsub1 : Subsingleton (Abelian.Ext (Φ.functor.obj (jShriekOU V)) (Φ.functor.obj H) q) :=
346	    subsingleton_ext_of_iso_fst hjt (Φ.functor.obj H) q

351

354	The argument is pure corepresentability transport — `jShriekOU V` is never unfolded.  Both sides
355	corepresent the *same* `Type`-valued sections functor `Γ(φ.inv⁻¹V,-)`: the right side directly (the

360	/-- **`jShriekOU V` corepresents the `Type`-valued sections functor `Γ(V,-)`.**  Repackages the

362	post-composed with the forgetful functor to types. Project-local. -/

365	  homEquiv {B} := (jShriekOU_homEquiv V B).toEquiv
366	  homEquiv_comp g f := jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat V f g

369	adjunction `Φ.functor ⊣ Φ.inverse` and the definitional identity "sections of `φ.inv`-pushforward
370	over `V` = sections over `φ.inv⁻¹V`", the object `Φ.functor (jShriekOU V)` corepresents the same
371	`Type`-valued sections functor as `jShriekOU (φ.inv⁻¹V)`.  Project-local. -/

374	    (sectionsFunctor (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ V) ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat).CorepresentableBy
375	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj (jShriekOU V)) where

387	For `φ : X ≅ Y` and an open `V ⊆ X`, the pushforward equivalence sends the corepresenting object of

397	/- Planner strategy:
398	Part (1): `R^q j_* H` is the sheafification of `V ↦ H^q(j⁻¹V, H)` (presheaf description
399	`higher_direct_image_presheaf`, Stacks 01XJ); for affine `V`, `j⁻¹V = U ∩ V` is affine (U affine,
400	X separated), so `affine_serre_vanishing` kills `H^q` for `q ≥ 1`; affine opens are a basis ⇒
401	`R^q j_* H = 0`. Part (2): take an injective resolution `H → I•`; `j_* I•` is a resolution of
402	`j_* H` (each `j_* Iⁿ` is `j_*`-acyclic by (1)) and each `j_* Iⁿ` is `f_*`-acyclic (same presheaf
403	+ Serre-vanishing argument on `U ∩ f⁻¹V`); so `j_* I•` is an `f_*`-acyclic resolution of `j_* H`,
404	and the P4 acyclic-resolution comparison (`acyclic_resolution_computes_derived` /
405	`rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`) with `G = f_*` gives `R^k f_*(j_* H) ≅ H^k(f_*(j_* I•)) =
406	H^k((f∘j)_* I•) = R^k (f∘j)_* H`, using `f_* ∘ j_* = (f∘j)_*`. Blueprint:
407	`lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`. Source: Stacks `lemma-relative-affine-vanishing`.
408	-/
409	
410	/-- **Higher direct images of an affine open immersion vanish** (part (1) of blueprint
411	`lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`; Stacks `lemma-relative-affine-vanishing`).
412	
413	For an open immersion `j : U ↪ X` where `U` is affine and `X` is separated, the morphism
414	`j` is affine (since for any affine open `V ⊆ X`, `j⁻¹V = U ∩ V` is affine by separatedness).
415	Therefore `R^q j_* H = 0` for every `q ≥ 1` and every quasi-coherent `O_U`-module `H`.
416	
417	Proof route: by `higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology`, `R^q j_* H` is the
418	sheafification of `V ↦ H^q(j⁻¹V, H)`. For affine `V`, `j⁻¹V = U ∩ V` is affine (`U` affine
419	+ `X` separated), and `affine_serre_vanishing` kills `H^q` for `q ≥ 1`. Since affine opens
420	form a basis, the presheaf homology is locally zero, and the site lemmas
421	`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective` (in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean`,
422	importable via `AffineSerreVanishing`) collapse the sheafification to zero. -/
423	private lemma isAffineHom_of_affine_separated (j : U ⟶ X) [IsAffine U] [X.IsSeparated] :
424	    IsAffineHom j := by
425	  have hg : IsSeparated (terminal.from X) := Scheme.IsSeparated.isSeparated_terminal_from
426	  have hcomp : IsAffineHom (j ≫ terminal.from X) := by
427	    have he : j ≫ terminal.from X = terminal.from U := terminal.hom_ext _ _
428	    rw [he]; infer_instance
429	  exact IsAffineHom.of_comp j (terminal.from X)
430	
431	/-- The sections-over-`j⁻¹W` functor on `U`-modules, `M ↦ Γ(W, j_* M) = Γ(j⁻¹W, M)`,
432	realised as the composite `pushforward j ⋙ forget ⋙ restrictScalars 𝟙 ⋙ toPresheaf ⋙ eval(W)`.
433	Its `q`-th right derived functor applied to `H` is the absolute cohomology `Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)`.
434	Project-local: a named composite so its additivity instance is synthesised once at the top
435	level (the 5-fold composite defeats in-proof instance search). -/
436	noncomputable def pushforwardSectionsFunctor (j : U ⟶ X) (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
437	    U.Modules ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
438	  Scheme.Modules.pushforward j ⋙ SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
439	    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj) ⋙
440	    PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
441	    (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)
442	
443	noncomputable instance pushforwardSectionsFunctor_additive
444	    (j : U ⟶ X) (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : (pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W).Additive := by
445	  unfold pushforwardSectionsFunctor
446	  -- The flat 5-fold composite defeats instance search; build it as an explicit `instAdditiveComp`
447	  -- chain (every individual factor is additive).
448	  haveI hpf : (Scheme.Modules.pushforward j).Additive := inferInstance
449	  haveI i4 : (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
450	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)).Additive :=
451	    Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
452	  haveI i3 : (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj) ⋙
453	      PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
454	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)).Additive :=
455	    Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
456	  haveI i2 : (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
457	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj) ⋙
458	      PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
459	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)).Additive :=
460	    Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
461	  -- Instance search will not pick up `i2` automatically here, so we pass it explicitly.
462	  exact @CategoryTheory.Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _ _ _ _ _
463	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward j) hpf _ _ _ _ i2
464	
465	theorem higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic [HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules]
466	    (j : U ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion j] [IsAffine U] [X.IsSeparated]
467	    (H : U.Modules) (hH : H.IsQuasicoherent) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) :
468	    IsZero (higherDirectImage j q H) := by
469	  -- `j` is an affine morphism (open immersion of an affine into a separated scheme),
470	  -- so the preimage of every affine open `V ⊆ X` is the affine open `j⁻¹V = U ∩ V`.
471	  haveI : IsAffineHom j := isAffineHom_of_affine_separated j
472	  -- Presheaf description (Stacks 01XJ): `Rᵠ j_* H ≅ sheafify(V ↦ Hᵠ((j_* I•)(V)))`
473	  -- for any injective resolution `I` of `H`. Reduce to the vanishing of that sheafification.
474	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_
475	    (higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology j q (injectiveResolution H))
476	  set α : X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ X.ringCatSheaf.obj := 𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj with hα
477	  set P := (pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex j (injectiveResolution H)).homology q with hP
478	  -- Reflect `IsZero` through the faithful, zero-preserving forgetful functor `toSheaf`.
479	  apply Functor.isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms (SheafOfModules.toSheaf X.ringCatSheaf)
480	  -- Transport across the sheafification square
481	  -- `toSheaf ∘ sheafification ≅ presheafToSheaf ∘ toPresheaf`.
482	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf α).app P)
483	  set Q := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj P with hQ
484	  -- KEY VANISHING (genuine cohomological gap): the presheaf homology `Q` is a *zero object*
485	  -- over every affine open `W ⊆ X`, because `j⁻¹W` is affine (`j` is an affine morphism) and
486	  -- `affine_serre_vanishing` kills `Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)` for `q ≥ 1`.
487	  have hSec : ∀ (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X), IsAffineOpen W →
488	      IsZero (Q.obj (Opposite.op W)) := by
489	    intro W hW
490	    -- Evaluate-at-`W` the presheaf complex; this functor preserves homology, so
491	    -- `Q.obj (op W) ≅ Hᵠ(Γ(W, f_* I•)) = Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)`, which vanishes for affine `j⁻¹W`.
492	    set GW := PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
493	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W) with hGW
494	    set Kp := pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex j (injectiveResolution H) with hKp
495	    refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ (GW.mapHomologyIso' (ComplexShape.up ℕ) Kp q).symm
496	    -- The section complex `n ↦ Γ(W, f_* I^n) = Γ(j⁻¹W, I^n)` is the image of the chosen
497	    -- injective resolution of `H` under the additive sections-over-`j⁻¹W` functor.
498	    have hcomplex : (GW.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj Kp
499	        = ((pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
500	            (injectiveResolution H).cocomplex :=
501	      rfl
502	    rw [hcomplex]
503	    -- `isoRightDerivedObj` recognises this homology as the `q`-th right derived sections
504	    -- functor `Rᵠ Γ(j⁻¹W, -)` applied to `H`, i.e. the absolute cohomology `Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)`.
505	    refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((injectiveResolution H).isoRightDerivedObj
506	      (pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W) q).symm
507	    -- The sections functor `Γ(j⁻¹W, -)` (here `pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W`, which is
508	    -- definitionally `sectionsFunctor (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)`) is corepresented by `jShriekOU (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)`, so its
509	    -- `q`-th right derived functor agrees with that of `Hom(jShriekOU (j ⁻¹ᵁ W), -)`.  This
510	    -- discharges the *geometric* corepresentability half of Bridge (1)/(2).
511	    refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((rightDerivedNatIso (sectionsFunctorCorepIso (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)) q).app H)
512	    -- Bridge (1)/(2) HOMOLOGICAL remainder DISCHARGED: `isZero_coyoneda_rightDerived_of_forall_ext_eq_zero`
513	    -- (built above, axiom-clean) reduces the right-derived vanishing of `Hom(jShriekOU (j⁻¹W), -)` to
514	    -- the `Ext`-vanishing `∀ e : Ext (jShriekOU (j⁻¹W)) H q, e = 0`.
515	    refine isZero_coyoneda_rightDerived_of_forall_ext_eq_zero (jShriekOU (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)) H q hq ?_
516	    intro e
517	    -- RESIDUAL (Bridge (2) Serre vanishing, geometric transport).  This goal `e = 0` is exactly the
518	    -- conclusion of the assembled `ext_jShriekOU_eq_zero_of_specIso U U.isoSpec (j ⁻¹ᵁ W) H q hq V'
519	    -- hV' hjt hqc e` (built above, axiom-clean), where `V'` is the affine image open of `j⁻¹W` in
520	    -- `Spec Γ(U,⊤)`.  The three remaining geometric facts are the only unbuilt pieces:
521	    --   • `hV' : IsAffineOpen V'`        — `j⁻¹W` affine (affine morphism `j`) + image under the iso;
522	    --   • `hjt : Φ(jShriekOU (j⁻¹W)) ≅ jShriekOU V'`  — the `jShriekOU` transport iso
523	    --       (`pushforward_commutes_free`/`_sheafify` + `yoneda_transport_along_homeo`);
524	    --   • `hqc : (Φ H).IsQuasicoherent`  — pushforward along an iso preserves quasi-coherence.
525	    -- `hV'` is discharged below; `hjt`/`hqc` are the two remaining geometric transport sub-lemmas.
526	    refine ext_jShriekOU_eq_zero_of_specIso U U.isoSpec (j ⁻¹ᵁ W) H q hq
527	      (U.isoSpec.inv ⁻¹ᵁ (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)) ?hV' ?hjt ?hqc e
528	    -- `j⁻¹W` is affine (`j` is an affine morphism, `W` affine), and its image under the iso
529	    -- `U.isoSpec.inv` (= preimage along `U.isoSpec.inv`) is affine.
530	    case hV' => exact (hW.preimage j).preimage_of_isIso U.isoSpec.inv
531	    case hjt => exact jShriekOU_transport_along_iso U.isoSpec (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)
532	    case hqc => sorry
533	  -- Since affine opens form a basis of `X`, every section of `Q` restricts to `0` on the
534	  -- affine opens contained in its domain, so `Q` is sectionwise locally zero and its
535	  -- sheafification vanishes.
536	  apply CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_sections_locally_zero
537	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Q := Q)
538	  intro U s
539	  refine ⟨{ arrows := fun {V} _ => ∃ W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X,
540	              IsAffineOpen W ∧ V ≤ W ∧ W ≤ U,
541	            downward_closed := ?_ }, ?_, ?_⟩
542	  · rintro V V' g ⟨A, hA, hVA, hAU⟩ h
543	    exact ⟨A, hA, le_trans (leOfHom h) hVA, hAU⟩
544	  · -- membership in the opens topology: every point of `U` has an affine basis neighbourhood
545	    intro x hx
546	    obtain ⟨W, hWmem, hxW, hWU⟩ :=
547	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.isBasis_iff_nbhd.mp (Scheme.isBasis_affineOpens X)) hx
548	    exact ⟨W, homOfLE hWU, ⟨W, hWmem, le_refl W, hWU⟩, hxW⟩
549	  · -- the restriction of `s` to a sieve member `V ≤ A ≤ U` (with `A` affine) factors through
550	    -- `Q.obj (op A) = 0`, hence vanishes.
551	    rintro V g ⟨A, hA, hVA, hAU⟩
552	    haveI : Subsingleton (ToType (Q.obj (Opposite.op A))) :=
553	      AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (hSec A hA)
554	    have hgfac : g.op = (homOfLE hAU).op ≫ (homOfLE hVA).op := by
555	      rw [← op_comp]; congr 1
556	    rw [hgfac, Q.map_comp, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
557	      Subsingleton.elim ((ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map (homOfLE hAU).op)) s) 0, map_zero]
558	
559	/-- **Composition formula for higher direct images across an open immersion** (pinned
560	declaration; blueprint `lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`, part (2); Stacks
561	`lemma-relative-affine-vanishing`).
562	
563	Let `j : U ↪ X` be an open immersion of an affine open `U` into a separated scheme `X`,
564	and `f : X ⟶ S` any morphism. For every quasi-coherent `O_U`-module `H` and every `k ≥ 0`:
565	```
566	  R^k f_*(j_* H) ≅ R^k (f ∘ j)_* H.
567	```
568	Proof route: choose an injective resolution `H → I•` in `U.Modules`; apply `j_*`
569	degreewise to get an `f_*`-acyclic resolution `j_* I•` of `j_* H`
570	(`higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic` for `j_*`-acyclicity, extended to
571	`f_*`-acyclicity via the same Serre-vanishing argument on `U ∩ f⁻¹V`); then
572	`Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` with `G = (pushforward f)` and the identity
573	`(pushforward f) ∘ (pushforward j) = pushforward (j ≫ f)` give the stated isomorphism. -/
574	noncomputable def higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp
575	    [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules] [HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules]
576	    (j : U ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion j] [IsAffine U] [X.IsSeparated]
577	    (f : X ⟶ S) (H : U.Modules) (hH : H.IsQuasicoherent) (k : ℕ) :
578	    higherDirectImage f k ((pushforward j).obj H) ≅
579	      higherDirectImage (j ≫ f) k H := by
580	  haveI : IsAffineHom j := isAffineHom_of_affine_separated j
581	  -- Structural plan (all categorical building blocks below are verified to exist):
582	  --   • `G := Scheme.Modules.pushforward f` is additive and preserves finite limits
583	  --     (`inferInstance`), so `Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` applies to it.
584	  --   • The pushed-forward injective resolution `K := (pushforward j).mapHomologicalComplex I•`
585	  --     is a candidate acyclic resolution of `j_* H = (pushforward j).obj H`.
586	  --   • `pushforwardComp j f : pushforward j ⋙ pushforward f ≅ pushforward (j ≫ f)` turns
587	  --     `H^k(G(K))` into `H^k((j ≫ f)_* I•) = R^k (j ≫ f)_* H`.
588	  -- RESIDUAL (genuine cohomological gaps, handed off — each depends on Part (1)'s residual):
589	  --   (a) `K` is exact in positive degrees and resolves `j_* H` (i.e. `j_* I•` is a
590	  --       resolution of `j_* H`) — this is `j_*`-acyclicity, the content of
591	  --       `higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic` applied to each `Iⁿ` and to `H`.
592	  --   (b) each term `(pushforward j).obj (Iⁿ)` is `(pushforward f)`-acyclic
593	  --       (`Functor.IsRightAcyclic`), i.e. `R^k f_*(j_* Iⁿ) = 0` for `k ≥ 1` — the same
594	  --       Serre-vanishing argument on the affine `U ∩ f⁻¹V` (generalises Part (1) from
595	  --       `j_*` to `f_*` via the presheaf description on `f⁻¹V`).
596	  -- Then `Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution (pushforward f) K (j_* H) e (a) k`
597	  -- composed with the `pushforwardComp` transport gives the stated iso.
598	  sorry
599	
600	end AlgebraicGeometry
601

content:
# Analogy: does Mathlib carry `pushforward_commutes_restriction`, and is `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` / `over` the right API?

## Mode
api-alignment

## Slug
pushforward-restriction

## Iteration
061

## Question
Build `AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_commutes_restriction`: for `φ : X ≅ Y`, `Φ =
Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ`, `H : X.Modules`, `U_i : Opens X`, `V_i = φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ U_i`,
and `e_i` the homeomorphism-induced slice-module equivalence,
`e_i.functor.obj (H.over U_i) ≅ (Φ.functor.obj H).over V_i`
(i.e. `(f_*H)|_V ≅ (f|_{f⁻¹V})_*(H|_{f⁻¹V})` for `f = φ` iso). Confirm whether Mathlib already
provides this commutation, whether `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` is the canonical tool, and
whether `Scheme.Modules.over W` is the right "restrict to an open" abstraction.

## Where this is consumed
`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/OpenImmersionPushforward.lean:532` — the lone `case hqc => sorry` in
`higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic`. `hqc : (Φ.functor.obj H).IsQuasicoherent`, the R1 core of
the unbuilt `isQuasicoherent_pushforwardEquivOfIso` (see `analogies/need1-transport.md`).

## Project artifact(s)
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/OpenImmersionPushforward.lean:204` — `Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ` (`Φ.functor = Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom`).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/OpenImmersionPushforward.lean:526-532` — the `ext_jShriekOU_eq_zero_of_specIso … ?hqc` call; `hqc` is the only open hole.
- No project-local `def over` for `Scheme.Modules` — `H.over U` is Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.over` (verified: grep finds no redefinition).

## Decisions identified

### Decision 1: Does Mathlib carry the comparison iso / qcoh-preservation under pushforward?

- **Mathlib idiom / state**: Mathlib carries **neither** the comparison iso **nor** any
  `IsQuasicoherent`-preservation lemma for `pushforward` / `pullback` / `restrict` / iso. Verified
  this iter against the project's pinned Mathlib:
  - The **only** `IsQuasicoherent` *instance* in all of Mathlib is `tilde`
    (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Tilde.lean:394`).
  - The **only** non-trivial *constructor* is `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop`
    (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/Quasicoherent.lean:377`) plus its engine
    `QuasicoherentData.bind` (`Quasicoherent.lean:360`) and same-site `QuasicoherentData.ofIsIso`
    (`Quasicoherent.lean:323`). `grep IsQuasicoherent` over `Mathlib/` returns no pushforward /
    pullback / restrict / open-immersion preservation lemma.
  - No `over`-vs-`pushforward` commutation lemma exists in `PushforwardContinuous.lean`.
- **Project's path**: build the comparison iso from `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` +
  `Scheme.Modules.pushforward_obj_obj` (rfl on sections), then feed `of_coversTop`.
- **Gap**: divergent-but-mandatory — there is nothing to align *to*; the content does not exist
  upstream. The project's path reuses the correct Mathlib *machinery*.
- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL**. (Genuine upstream gap, not a project failure.)

### Decision 2: Is `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` the canonical tool, or is there a slicker idiom?

- **Mathlib idiom**: `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`
  (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:305`) is precisely the tool
  Mathlib *itself* uses for exactly this transport: `QuasicoherentData.bind`'s `presentation` field
  (`Quasicoherent.lean:369-375`) builds `e := pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence (Over.iteratedSliceEquiv …)`
  then `(P.map e.inverse (.refl _)).ofIsIso (e.fullyFaithfulFunctor.preimageIso (e.counitIso.app …)).hom`.
  The project's R1 is the *same shape* with the homeomorphism-induced opens-site equivalence in place
  of `Over.iteratedSliceEquiv`.
- **Why the equivalence is NECESSARY (no `pushforwardComp`-only shortcut)**: `Presentation.map`
  consumes a **colimit-preserving** functor between the slice module categories (generators/relations
  are colimits). A bare `SheafOfModules.pushforward` is a **right** adjoint (right adjoint to
  `pullback`) and does **not** preserve colimits, so it cannot transport a `Presentation`. Only the
  full *equivalence* (whose functor preserves all (co)limits) works. This is exactly why `bind` uses
  the equivalence and not a bare pushforward. Hence there is **no slicker single-`pushforwardComp`
  idiom** that avoids the over-site equivalence assembly.
- **The comparison ISO itself is short** (the expensive part is *not* the iso): it is the
  `restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso` recipe — `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean:335` builds
  `pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭` as
  `pushforwardComp ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso (opens-equality) ≪≫ pushforwardCongr (ring-map equality) ≪≫ pushforwardId`,
  where `SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp` (`PushforwardContinuous.lean:101`) is **definitionally
  `Iso.refl`** and `pushforward_obj_obj` is `rfl`. The directive's comparison iso is the analogous
  four-step composite at object `H`.
- **Where the ~100-150 LOC actually lives (irreducible via this route)**: assembling the *site*
  equivalence `Over U_i ≌ Over V_i` from the homeomorphism `φ`, supplying its `IsContinuous`
  instances, and discharging `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`'s two coherence hyps `H₁ H₂`
  (in `bind` these are `by ext : 2; exact R.1.map_id _`-cheap; here `𝟙`-ring-map + structure-sheaf
  identifications, similarly cheap). Building blocks that exist:
  `Over.map` / `Over.forget` / `Over.star` are continuous (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Over.lean:283/243/335`);
  `Opens.map` of an (open-embedding/homeomorphism) base map is continuous
  (`Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Functors.lean:126`, `IsOpenEmbedding.functor_isContinuous`).
- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** with `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` — it is the canonical *and
  necessary* tool. No cheaper idiom exists.

### Decision 3: Is `Scheme.Modules.over W` the right abstraction, or a parallel API?

- **Mathlib idiom**: `SheafOfModules.over M X := (pushforward (𝟙 _)).obj M`
  (`PushforwardContinuous.lean:53`) — restriction to the *slice site* `Over X`. This is the **exact**
  abstraction Mathlib's qcoh layer is defined on: `QuasicoherentData.presentation (i) :
  (M.over (X i)).Presentation` (`Quasicoherent.lean:201,208`). Proving `IsQuasicoherent (Φ H)` via
  `of_coversTop` *forces* landing `IsQuasicoherent ((Φ H).over V_i)` per cover element.
- **Project's path**: uses `H.over U` = Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.over` directly (no redefinition).
- **The genuine parallel-API trap to avoid**: Mathlib *also* has `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor` /
  `restrict` (`Sheaf.lean:319,325`) — restriction along an **open-immersion morphism of schemes**
  (`Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)`). That is a **different** (geometric-morphism) abstraction;
  using it to feed `IsQuasicoherent`/`of_coversTop` would be the parallel-API mistake, because the
  qcoh definition speaks the slice-site `over` language, not the scheme-morphism `restrict` language.
- **Gap**: identical — the project already uses the canonical `over`.
- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — keep `over`; do **not** re-model the open-restriction via
  `Scheme.Modules.restrict` for the qcoh assembly.

## Recommendation

**PROCEED** with the `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` route — it is the canonical *and* necessary
idiom (the colimit-preservation that `Presentation.map` demands rules out any bare-`pushforward`
shortcut), and `over` is the mandated abstraction. The single best alignment move is to **mirror
`QuasicoherentData.bind`'s `presentation` field almost verbatim**: state
`isQuasicoherent_pushforwardEquivOfIso {X Y} (φ : X ≅ Y) (H : X.Modules) [H.IsQuasicoherent] :
((pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj H).IsQuasicoherent`, prove it via `of_coversTop` over the
image cover `fun i => φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ (q.X i)`, and for each `i` build the per-slice equivalence
`e_i := pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence <homeo-induced Over U_i ≌ Over V_i> (𝟙) (𝟙) H₁ H₂`,
then `(q.presentation i).map e_i.inverse (.refl _)).ofIsIso <comparison>.hom` exactly as `bind`
does with `Over.iteratedSliceEquiv`. The comparison iso `e_i.functor.obj (H.over U_i) ≅ (Φ H).over V_i`

`pushforward_commutes_restriction` API divorced from `bind`'s shape, and do not route through
`Scheme.Modules.restrict`.

12	/-!
13	# Quasicoherent sheaves
14	
15	A sheaf of modules is quasi-coherent if it admits locally a presentation as the
16	cokernel of a morphism between coproducts of copies of the sheaf of rings.
17	When these coproducts are finite, we say that the sheaf is of finite presentation.
18	
19	## References
20	
21	* https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/01BD
22	
23	-/

31	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
32	  {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
33	
34	namespace SheafOfModules

36	section
37	
38	variable [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
39	  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
40	
41	/-- A global presentation of a sheaf of modules `M` consists of a family `generators.s`
42	of sections `s` which generate `M`, and a family of sections which generate
43	the kernel of the morphism `generators.π : free (generators.I) ⟶ M`. -/
44	structure Presentation (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) where
45	  /-- generators -/
46	  generators : M.GeneratingSections
47	  /-- relations -/
48	  relations : (kernel generators.π).GeneratingSections
49	
50	/-- A global presentation of a sheaf of module if finite if the type
51	of generators and relations are finite. -/
52	class Presentation.IsFinite {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (p : M.Presentation) : Prop where
53	  isFiniteType_generators : p.generators.IsFiniteType := by infer_instance
54	  isFiniteType_relations : p.relations.IsFiniteType := by infer_instance
55	
56	attribute [instance] Presentation.IsFinite.isFiniteType_generators
57	  Presentation.IsFinite.isFiniteType_relations
58	
59	@[deprecated Presentation.IsFinite.isFiniteType_relations (since := "2026-04-14")]
60	lemma Presentation.IsFinite.finite_relations {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (p : M.Presentation)
61	    [p.IsFinite] : Finite p.relations.I := GeneratingSections.IsFiniteType.finite
62	
63	end

72	satisfying `H : f ≫ g = 0` and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`, we obtain
73	generators of `Presentation M`. -/
74	@[simps! I s]
75	def generatorsOfIsCokernelFree {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R}
76	    (f : free ι ⟶ free σ) (g : free σ ⟶ M) (H : f ≫ g = 0)
77	    (H' : IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)) : M.GeneratingSections where
78	  I := σ
79	  s := M.freeHomEquiv g
80	  epi := by simpa using epi_of_isColimit_cofork H'

84	    (f : free ι ⟶ free σ) (g : free σ ⟶ M) (H : f ≫ g = 0)
85	    (H' : IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)) :
86	    (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π = g := M.freeHomEquiv.symm_apply_apply g

90	satisfying `H : f ≫ g = 0` and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`, we obtain
91	relations of `Presentation M`. -/

94	    (f : free ι ⟶ free σ) (g : free σ ⟶ M) (H : f ≫ g = 0)

96	    (kernel (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π).GeneratingSections where
97	  I := ι

99	    (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π f (by simp [H])

102	    let h' : Abelian.image f ≅ kernel (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π :=
103	      kernel.mapIso (cokernel.π f) (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π
104	        (Iso.refl _) h (by simp [h])
105	    have comp_aux : Abelian.factorThruImage f ≫ h'.hom =
106	      (kernel.lift (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π f (by simp [H])) :=
107	        equalizer.hom_ext <| by simp [h']
108	    rw [← comp_aux, Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
109	    infer_instance

111	/-- Given two morphisms of sheaves of `R`-modules `f : free ι ⟶ free σ` and `g : free σ ⟶ M`
112	satisfying `H : f ≫ g = 0` and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`, we obtain a
113	`Presentation M`. -/

116	    (f : free ι ⟶ free σ) (g : free σ ⟶ M) (H : f ≫ g = 0)
117	    (H' : IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)) : Presentation M where
118	  generators := generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H'
119	  relations := relationsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H'

123	and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`. -/
124	def Presentation.isColimit {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (P : Presentation M) :
125	    IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ (f := (freeHomEquiv _).symm P.relations.s ≫ (kernel.ι _))
126	      P.generators.π (by simp)) :=
127	  isCokernelEpiComp (c := CokernelCofork.ofπ _ (kernel.condition P.generators.π))
128	      (Abelian.epiIsCokernelOfKernel _ <| limit.isLimit _) _ rfl

133	    (σ : M.Presentation) : N.Presentation where
134	  generators := σ.generators.ofEpi f
135	  relations := σ.relations.ofEpi ((kernelCompMono _ f).symm.trans <| eqToIso (by simp)).hom

142	  isFiniteType_relations := inferInstanceAs (σ.relations.ofEpi _).IsFiniteType

199	/-- This structure contains the data of a family of objects `X i` which cover
200	the terminal object, and of a presentation of `M.over (X i)` for all `i`. -/
201	structure QuasicoherentData (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) where
202	  /-- the index type of the covering -/
203	  I : Type w
204	  /-- a family of objects which cover the terminal object -/
205	  X : I → C
206	  coversTop : J.CoversTop X
207	  /-- a presentation of the sheaf of modules `M.over (X i)` for any `i : I` -/
208	  presentation (i : I) : (M.over (X i)).Presentation
209	
210	namespace QuasicoherentData
211	
212	/-- Shrink the indexing type of `QuasicoherentData` into the universe of the site. -/
213	noncomputable
214	def shrink {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (q : M.QuasicoherentData) :
215	    QuasicoherentData.{u₁} M where
216	  I := Set.range q.X
217	  X i := q.X i.2.choose
218	  coversTop X := by
219	    refine J.superset_covering (fun Y hY H ↦ ?_) (q.coversTop X)
220	    obtain ⟨i, ⟨hi⟩⟩ := (Sieve.mem_ofObjects_iff ..).mp H
221	    exact ⟨⟨_, i, rfl⟩, ⟨hi ≫ eqToHom (by grind)⟩⟩
222	  presentation i := q.presentation i.2.choose
223	
224	/-- If `M` is quasicoherent, it is locally generated by sections. -/
225	@[simps]
226	def localGeneratorsData {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (q : M.QuasicoherentData) :
227	    M.LocalGeneratorsData where
228	  I := q.I
229	  X := q.X
230	  coversTop := q.coversTop
231	  generators i := (q.presentation i).generators
232	
233	/-- A (local) presentation of a sheaf of module `M` is a finite presentation
234	if each given presentation of `M.over (X i)` is a finite presentation. -/
235	class IsFinitePresentation {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (q : M.QuasicoherentData) : Prop where
236	  isFinite_presentation (i : q.I) : (q.presentation i).IsFinite := by infer_instance
237	
238	attribute [instance] IsFinitePresentation.isFinite_presentation
239	
240	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
241	instance {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (q : M.QuasicoherentData) [q.IsFinitePresentation] :
242	    q.localGeneratorsData.IsFiniteType where
243	  isFiniteType := by dsimp; infer_instance
244	
245	end QuasicoherentData
246	
247	/-- A sheaf of modules is quasi-coherent if it is locally the cokernel of a
248	morphism between coproducts of copies of the sheaf of rings. -/
249	class IsQuasicoherent (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) : Prop where
250	  nonempty_quasicoherentData : Nonempty (QuasicoherentData.{u₁} M) := by infer_instance
251	
252	lemma QuasicoherentData.isQuasicoherent {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (q : M.QuasicoherentData) :
253	    M.IsQuasicoherent := ⟨⟨q.shrink⟩⟩
254	
255	variable (R) in
256	@[inherit_doc IsQuasicoherent]
257	abbrev isQuasicoherent : ObjectProperty (SheafOfModules.{u} R) :=
258	  IsQuasicoherent
259	
260	/-- A sheaf of modules is finitely presented if it is locally the cokernel of a
261	morphism between coproducts of finitely many copies of the sheaf of rings. -/
262	class IsFinitePresentation (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) : Prop where
263	  exists_quasicoherentData (M) :
264	    ∃ (σ : QuasicoherentData.{u₁} M), σ.IsFinitePresentation
265	
266	variable (R) in
267	@[inherit_doc IsFinitePresentation]
268	abbrev isFinitePresentation : ObjectProperty (SheafOfModules.{u} R) :=
269	  IsFinitePresentation
270	
271	instance (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) [M.IsFinitePresentation] :
272	    M.IsQuasicoherent where
273	  nonempty_quasicoherentData :=
274	    ⟨(IsFinitePresentation.exists_quasicoherentData M).choose⟩
275	
276	instance (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) [M.IsFinitePresentation] :
277	    M.IsFiniteType where
278	  exists_localGeneratorsData := by
279	    obtain ⟨σ, _⟩ := IsFinitePresentation.exists_quasicoherentData M
280	    exact ⟨σ.localGeneratorsData, inferInstance⟩
281	
282	/-- A choice of local presentations when `M` is a sheaf of modules of finite presentation. -/
283	@[deprecated "Use the lemma `IsFinitePresentation.exists_quasicoherentData` instead."
284	  (since := "2025-10-28")]
285	noncomputable def quasicoherentDataOfIsFinitePresentation
286	    (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) [M.IsFinitePresentation] : M.QuasicoherentData :=
287	  (IsFinitePresentation.exists_quasicoherentData M).choose
288	
289	end
290	
291	noncomputable section
292	
293	open CategoryTheory Limits
294	
295	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] [HasBinaryProducts C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
296	  {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat]
297	  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)]
298	
299	variable [∀ X, (J.over X).HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)]
300	  [∀ X, HasSheafify (J.over X) AddCommGrpCat]
301	  [∀ X, (J.over X).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat]
302	
303	/-- Given a sheaf of `R`-modules `M` and a `Presentation M`, we may construct the quasi-coherent
304	data on the trivial cover. -/
305	@[simps]
306	def Presentation.quasicoherentData {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (P : Presentation M) :
307	    QuasicoherentData M where
308	  I := C
309	  X := id
310	  coversTop x := GrothendieckTopology.covering_of_eq_top J <| by
311	    rw [Sieve.ext_iff]
312	    intro _ f
313	    simpa [Sieve.top_apply, iff_true] using ⟨x, Nonempty.intro f⟩
314	  presentation x := P.map (pushforward (𝟙 (R.over x))) (by rfl)
315	
316	/-- If a sheaf of `R`-modules `M` has a presentation, then `M` is quasi-coherent. -/
317	theorem Presentation.isQuasicoherent {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (P : Presentation M) :
318	    IsQuasicoherent M where
319	  nonempty_quasicoherentData := Nonempty.intro (Presentation.quasicoherentData P)
320	
321	/-- Mapping quasicoherent data under an isomorphism. -/
322	@[simps]
323	noncomputable def QuasicoherentData.ofIsIso {M N : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : M ⟶ N) [IsIso f]
324	    (σ : M.QuasicoherentData) : N.QuasicoherentData where
325	  I := σ.I
326	  X := σ.X
327	  coversTop := σ.coversTop
328	  presentation i := Presentation.ofIsIso (f.over (σ.X i)) (σ.presentation i)
329	
330	instance : (isQuasicoherent R).IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms where
331	  of_iso e := by
332	    intro ⟨⟨q⟩⟩
333	    exact ⟨⟨q.ofIsIso e.hom⟩⟩
334	
335	instance {M N : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : M ⟶ N) [IsIso f] (σ : M.QuasicoherentData)
336	    [σ.IsFinitePresentation] : (σ.ofIsIso f).IsFinitePresentation where
337	  isFinite_presentation i := by
338	    dsimp
339	    exact inferInstanceAs ((σ.presentation i).ofIsIso _).IsFinite
340	
341	instance : (isFinitePresentation R).IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms where
342	  of_iso e := by
343	    intro ⟨σ, hσ⟩
344	    exact ⟨σ.ofIsIso e.hom, inferInstance⟩
345	
346	end
347	
348	section bind
349	
350	variable [∀ X, (J.over X).HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
351	  [∀ X, HasSheafify (J.over X) AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
352	  [∀ X, (J.over X).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
353	  [∀ X Y, ((J.over X).over Y).HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
354	  [∀ X Y, HasSheafify ((J.over X).over Y) AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
355	  [∀ X Y, ((J.over X).over Y).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
356	
357	#adaptation_note /-- After nightly-2026-02-23 we need this to avoid timeouts. -/
358	/-- Given an cover `X` and a quasicoherent data for `M` restricted onto each `Mᵢ`, we may glue them
359	into a quasicoherent data of `M` itself. -/
360	noncomputable def QuasicoherentData.bind {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
361	    (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) {I : Type u}
362	    (X : I → C) (hX : J.CoversTop X) (D : Π i, QuasicoherentData (M.over (X i))) :
363	    M.QuasicoherentData where
364	  I := Σ i, (D i).I
365	  X ij := ((D ij.1).X ij.2).left
366	  coversTop Y := J.transitive (hX Y) _ fun Z f ⟨i, ⟨g⟩⟩ ↦
367	      J.superset_covering ((Sieve.functorPushforward_ofObjects_le _ _ _).trans
368	      (Sieve.ofObjects_mono fun i' ↦ by aesop)) ((D i).coversTop (.mk g))
369	  presentation i :=
370	    letI e := pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence (Over.iteratedSliceEquiv ((D i.1).X i.2))
371	      (S := (R.over _).over _) (R := R.over _) (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _)
372	      (by ext : 2; exact R.1.map_id _) (by ext : 2; exact R.1.map_id _)
373	    (((D i.1).presentation i.2).map e.inverse (.refl _)).ofIsIso
374	      (e.fullyFaithfulFunctor.preimageIso
375	      (by exact e.counitIso.app ((M.over (X i.1)).over ((D i.1).X i.2)))).hom
376	
377	lemma IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
378	    (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) {I : Type u}
379	    (X : I → C) (hX : J.CoversTop X) [∀ i, IsQuasicoherent (M.over (X i))] :
380	    IsQuasicoherent M :=
381	  (QuasicoherentData.bind M X hX fun _ ↦
382	    IsQuasicoherent.nonempty_quasicoherentData.some).isQuasicoherent
383	
384	end bind
385	
386	end SheafOfModules

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pushforward
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Over
11	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Comma.Over.Pullback
12	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.BinaryProducts

14	/-!
15	# Pushforward of sheaves of modules
16	
17	Assume that categories `C` and `D` are equipped with Grothendieck topologies, and
18	that `F : C ⥤ D` is a continuous functor.
19	Then, if `φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R` is
20	a morphism of sheaves of rings, we construct the pushforward functor
21	`pushforward φ : SheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} S`, and
22	we show that they interact with the composition of morphisms similarly as pseudofunctors.
23	
24	-/
25	
26	@[expose] public section
27	
28	universe w v' u' v v₁ v₂ v₃ v₄ u₁ u₂ u₃ u₄ u
29	
30	open CategoryTheory Functor
31	
32	namespace SheafOfModules
33	
34	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
35	  {D' : Type u₃} [Category.{v₃} D'] {D'' : Type u₄} [Category.{v₄} D'']
36	  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {F : C ⥤ D}
37	  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
38	  [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]
39	  (φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)
40	
41	/-- The pushforward of sheaves of modules that is induced by a continuous functor `F`
42	and a morphism of sheaves of rings `φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat J K).obj R`. -/
43	@[simps map_val, simps -isSimp obj_val]
44	noncomputable def pushforward : SheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} S where
45	  obj M :=
46	    { val := (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ.hom).obj M.val
47	      isSheaf := ((F.sheafPushforwardContinuous _ J K).obj ⟨_, M.isSheaf⟩).property }
48	  map f :=
49	    { val := (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ.hom).map f.val }
50	
51	/-- Given `M : SheafOfModules R` and `X : D`, this is the restriction of `M`
52	over the sheaf of rings `R.over X` on the category `Over X`. -/
53	noncomputable abbrev over (M : SheafOfModules.{v} R) (X : D) : SheafOfModules.{v} (R.over X) :=
54	  (pushforward.{v} (𝟙 _)).obj M
55	
56	/-- Given a map `f : M ⟶ N` between sheaves of modules over `R`, this is the restriction
57	to the map `M.over X ⟶ N.over X` between sheaves of modules over `R.over X`. -/
58	noncomputable abbrev Hom.over {M N : SheafOfModules.{v} R} (f : M ⟶ N) (X : D) :
59	    M.over X ⟶ N.over X :=
60	  (pushforward.{v} (𝟙 _)).map f
61	
62	section
63	
64	variable (R) in
65	/-- The pushforward functor by the identity morphism identifies to
66	the identify functor of the category of sheaves of modules. -/
67	noncomputable def pushforwardId :
68	    pushforward.{v} (S := R) (F := 𝟭 _) (𝟙 R) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
69	  Iso.refl _
70	
71	/-- Pushforwards along equal morphisms of sheaves of rings are isomorphic. -/
72	noncomputable
73	def pushforwardCongr {φ ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R} (e : φ = ψ) :
74	    pushforward.{v} φ ≅ pushforward.{v} ψ :=
75	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun X ↦ (SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _).preimageIso
76	    (PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun U ↦ (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr (by subst e; rfl)).app _)
77	      fun _ _ _ ↦ by subst e; rfl)) fun _ ↦ by subst e; rfl
78	
79	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_symm
80	    {φ ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R} (e : φ = ψ) :
81	  (pushforwardCongr e).symm = pushforwardCongr e.symm := rfl
82	
83	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_val_app
84	    {φ ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R} (e : φ = ψ) (M U x) :
85	  ((pushforwardCongr e).hom.app M).val.app U x = x := rfl
86	
87	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_val_app
88	    {φ ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R} (e : φ = ψ) (M U x) :
89	  ((pushforwardCongr e).inv.app M).val.app U x = x := rfl
90	
91	section
92	
93	variable {K' : GrothendieckTopology D'} {K'' : GrothendieckTopology D''}
94	  {G : D ⥤ D'} {R' : Sheaf K' RingCat.{u}}
95	  [Functor.IsContinuous G K K'] [Functor.IsContinuous (F ⋙ G) J K']
96	  (ψ : R ⟶ (G.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} K K').obj R')
97	
98	#adaptation_note /-- After nightly-2026-02-23 we need this to avoid timeouts. -/
99	/-- The composition of two pushforward functors on categories of sheaves of modules
100	identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
101	noncomputable def pushforwardComp :
102	    pushforward.{v} ψ ⋙ pushforward.{v} φ ≅
103	      pushforward.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).map ψ) :=
104	  Iso.refl _
105	
106	-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
107	lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_val_app (M U x) :
108	  ((pushforwardComp φ ψ).hom.app M).val.app U x = x := rfl
109	
110	-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
111	lemma pushforwardComp_inv_app_val_app (M U x) :
112	  ((pushforwardComp φ ψ).inv.app M).val.app U x = x := rfl
113	
114	variable {G' : D' ⥤ D''} {R'' : Sheaf K'' RingCat.{u}}
115	  [Functor.IsContinuous G' K' K'']
116	  [Functor.IsContinuous (G ⋙ G') K K'']
117	  [Functor.IsContinuous ((F ⋙ G) ⋙ G') J K'']
118	  [Functor.IsContinuous (F ⋙ G ⋙ G') J K'']
119	  (ψ' : R' ⟶ (G'.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} K' K'').obj R'')
120	
121	lemma pushforward_assoc :
122	    (pushforward ψ').isoWhiskerLeft (pushforwardComp φ ψ) ≪≫
123	      pushforwardComp (F := F ⋙ G)
124	        (φ ≫ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).map ψ) ψ' =
125	    ((pushforward ψ').associator (pushforward ψ) (pushforward φ)).symm ≪≫
126	      isoWhiskerRight (pushforwardComp ψ ψ') (pushforward φ) ≪≫
127	      pushforwardComp (G := G ⋙ G') φ (ψ ≫
128	        (G.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} K K').map ψ') := by ext; rfl
129	
130	end
131	
132	lemma pushforward_comp_id :
133	    pushforwardComp.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) φ =
134	      isoWhiskerLeft (pushforward.{v} φ) (pushforwardId S) ≪≫ rightUnitor _ := by ext; rfl
135	
136	lemma pushforward_id_comp :
137	    pushforwardComp.{v} (G := 𝟭 _) φ (𝟙 R) =
138	      isoWhiskerRight (pushforwardId R) (pushforward.{v} φ) ≪≫ leftUnitor _ := by ext; rfl
139	
140	end
141	
142	section NatTrans
143	
144	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
145	  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D}
146	  {F G H : C ⥤ D} {T : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {S : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
147	  [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]
148	  [Functor.IsContinuous G J K]
149	  [Functor.IsContinuous H J K]
150	  (φ : T ⟶ (G.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj S)
151	
152	/-- A natural transformation gives a natural transformation between the pushforward functors. -/
153	noncomputable
154	def pushforwardNatTrans (α : F ⟶ G) :
155	    pushforward.{v} φ ⟶
156	      pushforward.{v} (φ ≫ (Functor.sheafPushforwardContinuousNatTrans α _ _ _).app S) where
157	  app X :=
158	  { val.app U := (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.hom.app U).hom).map (X.val.map (α.app U.unop).op)
159	    val.naturality {U V} i := by
160	      ext x
161	      dsimp
162	      change (X.val.presheaf.map (G.map i.unop).op ≫ X.val.presheaf.map (α.app V.unop).op) _ =
163	        (X.val.presheaf.map (α.app U.unop).op ≫ X.val.presheaf.map (F.map i.unop).op) _
164	      simp only [← CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, α.naturality] }
165	  naturality {X Y} f := by
166	    ext U x
167	    exact congr($(f.val.naturality (α.app U.unop).op) x).symm
168	
169	@[simp] lemma pushforwardNatTrans_app_val_app (α : F ⟶ G) (M U x) :
170	    ((pushforwardNatTrans φ α).app M).val.app U x = M.val.map (α.app U.unop).op x := rfl
171	
172	@[simp]
173	lemma pushforwardNatTrans_id :
174	    pushforwardNatTrans φ (𝟙 G) = (pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)).hom := by cat_disch
175	
176	@[simp]
177	lemma pushforwardNatTrans_comp (α : F ⟶ G) (β : G ⟶ H)
178	    (φ : T ⟶ (H.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj S) :
179	    pushforwardNatTrans φ (α ≫ β) = pushforwardNatTrans φ β ≫ pushforwardNatTrans _ α ≫
180	      (pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)).hom := by cat_disch
181	
182	@[simp]
183	lemma pushforwardNatTrans_app_val_app_apply (α : F ⟶ G) (X U x) :
184	    ((pushforwardNatTrans φ α).app X).val.app U x = X.val.map (α.app U.unop).op x := rfl
185	
186	/-- A natural isomorphism gives a natural isomorphism between the pushforward functors. -/
187	@[simps]
188	noncomputable def pushforwardNatIso (α : F ≅ G) :
189	    pushforward.{v} φ ≅
190	      pushforward.{v} (φ ≫ (Functor.sheafPushforwardContinuousNatTrans α.hom _ _ _).app S) where
191	  hom := pushforwardNatTrans _ α.hom
192	  inv := pushforwardNatTrans _ α.inv ≫
193	    (pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp])).hom
194	  hom_inv_id := by
195	    ext X U x
196	    suffices X.val.presheaf.map (α.hom.app U.unop).op ≫
197	      X.val.presheaf.map (α.inv.app U.unop).op = 𝟙 _ from congr($this x)
198	    simp only [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp,
199	      Iso.inv_hom_id_app, op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
200	  inv_hom_id := by
201	    ext X U x
202	    suffices X.val.presheaf.map (α.inv.app U.unop).op ≫
203	      X.val.presheaf.map (α.hom.app U.unop).op = 𝟙 _ from congr($this x)
204	    simp only [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp,
205	      Iso.hom_inv_id_app, op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
206	
207	end NatTrans
208	
209	section Adjunction
210	
211	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
212	  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {F : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C}
213	  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
214	  [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]
215	  [Functor.IsContinuous G K J]
216	  (adj : F ⊣ G)
217	  (φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)
218	  (ψ : R ⟶ (G.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} K J).obj S)
219	  (H₁ : Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.counit) R.obj = ψ.hom ≫ G.op.whiskerLeft φ.hom)
220	  (H₂ : φ.hom ≫ F.op.whiskerLeft ψ.hom ≫
221	    Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.unit) S.obj = 𝟙 S.obj)
222	
223	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
224	/-- If `F ⊣ G`, then the pushforwards along `F` and `G` are also adjoint. -/
225	noncomputable
226	def pushforwardPushforwardAdj : pushforward.{v} φ ⊣ pushforward.{v} ψ where
227	  unit :=
228	    letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp G F K J K
229	    (pushforwardId _).inv ≫ pushforwardNatTrans (𝟙 _) adj.counit ≫
230	      (pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa)).hom ≫ (pushforwardComp _ _).inv
231	  counit :=
232	    letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp F G J K J
233	    (pushforwardComp _ _).hom ≫ pushforwardNatTrans _ adj.unit ≫
234	      (pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa)).hom ≫ (pushforwardId _).hom
235	  left_triangle_components X := by
236	    ext U x
237	    change (X.val.presheaf.map (adj.counit.app (F.obj U.unop)).op ≫
238	      X.val.presheaf.map (F.map (adj.unit.app U.unop)).op) _ = _
239	    dsimp only [id_obj]
240	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.left_triangle_components]
241	    simp
242	  right_triangle_components X := by
243	    ext U x
244	    change (X.val.presheaf.map (G.map (adj.counit.app U.unop)).op ≫
245	      X.val.presheaf.map (adj.unit.app (G.obj U.unop)).op) _ = _
246	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.right_triangle_components]
247	    simp
248	
249	-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
250	lemma pushforwardPushforwardAdj_unit_app_val_app (M U x) :
251	    ((pushforwardPushforwardAdj adj φ ψ H₁ H₂).unit.app M).val.app U x =
252	      M.val.map (adj.counit.app U.unop).op x := rfl
253	
254	-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
255	lemma pushforwardPushforwardAdj_counit_app_val_app (M U x) :
256	    ((pushforwardPushforwardAdj adj φ ψ H₁ H₂).counit.app M).val.app U x =
257	      M.val.map (adj.unit.app U.unop).op x := rfl
258	
259	noncomputable section
260	
261	open CategoryTheory Limits
262	
263	variable {C : Type u'} [Category.{v'} C] [HasBinaryProducts C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
264	  {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
265	
266	/-- The canonical morphism from `R` to the pushforward of its restriction to `Over x`. -/
267	def pushforwardOver (x : C) :
268	    R ⟶ ((Over.star x).sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat J (J.over x)).obj (R.over x) :=
269	  ⟨{app U := R.obj.map Limits.prod.snd.op
270	    naturality U V f := by simp [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp]; rfl }⟩
271	
272	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
273	/-- The adjunction between restriction to `Over x` and pushforward along `Over.star x`. -/
274	def overPushforwardOverAdj (x : C) :
275	    pushforward.{w} (𝟙 (R.over x)) ⊣ pushforward.{w} (pushforwardOver x) := by
276	  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (Over.forgetAdjStar x) (𝟙 (R.over x)) _ ?_ ?_
277	  · ext y : 2
278	    simp [pushforwardOver]
279	  · ext y : 2
280	    simp [pushforwardOver, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp]
281	
282	instance (x : C) : IsLeftAdjoint (pushforward.{w} (𝟙 (R.over x))) where
283	  exists_rightAdjoint := ⟨_, Nonempty.intro (overPushforwardOverAdj x)⟩
284	
285	end
286	
287	end Adjunction
288	
289	section Equivalence
290	
291	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
292	  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} (eqv : C ≌ D)
293	  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
294	  [Functor.IsContinuous eqv.functor J K]
295	  [Functor.IsContinuous eqv.inverse K J]
296	  (φ : S ⟶ (eqv.functor.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)
297	  (ψ : R ⟶ (eqv.inverse.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} K J).obj S)
298	  (H₁ : Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.counit) R.obj =
299	    ψ.hom ≫ eqv.inverse.op.whiskerLeft φ.hom)
300	  (H₂ : φ.hom ≫ eqv.functor.op.whiskerLeft ψ.hom ≫
301	    Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.unit) S.obj = 𝟙 S.obj)
302	
303	/-- If `e : C ≌ D`, then the pushforwards along `e.functor` and `e.inverse` forms an equivalence. -/
304	noncomputable
305	def pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence : SheafOfModules R ≌ SheafOfModules S where
306	  functor := pushforward.{v} φ
307	  inverse := pushforward.{v} ψ
308	  unitIso :=
309	    letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp eqv.inverse eqv.functor K J K
310	    (pushforwardId _).symm ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ eqv.counitIso ≪≫
311	      pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa) ≪≫ (pushforwardComp _ _).symm
312	  counitIso :=
313	    letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp eqv.functor eqv.inverse J K J
314	    pushforwardComp _ _ ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ eqv.unitIso ≪≫
315	      pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa) ≪≫ pushforwardId _
316	  functor_unitIso_comp :=
317	    (pushforwardPushforwardAdj eqv.toAdjunction φ ψ H₁ H₂).left_triangle_components
318	
319	-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
320	lemma pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_unit_app_val_app (M U x) :
321	    ((pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence eqv φ ψ H₁ H₂).unit.app M).val.app U x =
322	      M.val.map (eqv.counit.app U.unop).op x := rfl
323	
324	-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
325	lemma pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_counit_app_val_app (M U x) :
326	    ((pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence eqv φ ψ H₁ H₂).counit.app M).val.app U x =
327	      M.val.map (eqv.unit.app U.unop).op x := rfl
328	
329	end Equivalence
330	
331	end SheafOfModules
332

lemma inv_app [IsIso φ] : (inv φ).app U = inv (φ.app U) := by
  apply IsIso.eq_inv_of_hom_inv_id
  simp [← Hom.comp_app]

end Presheaf

noncomputable section Functorial

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (h : Z ⟶ T)

/-- The pushforward functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pushforward : X.Modules ⥤ Y.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_obj (M : X.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    Γ((pushforward f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_presheaf_map {U V : Y.Opens} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    ((pushforward f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map ((Opens.map f.base).map i).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_map_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforward f).map φ).app U = φ.app (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pullback : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes
is left adjoint to the pushforward functor. -/
def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback f ⊣ pushforward f :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _

section

attribute [local instance] preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts
  preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryProducts

instance : (pullback f).IsLeftAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).IsRightAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isRightAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
instance : (pullback f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _

end

variable (X) in
/-- The pushforward of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
to the identity functor. -/
def pushforwardId : pushforward (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_hom_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_inv_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl

variable (X) in
/-- The pullback of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
to the identity functor. -/
def pullbackId : pullback (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackId _

variable (X) in
lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom :
    conjugateEquiv .id (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 X)) (pullbackId X).hom =
      (pushforwardId X).inv :=
  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom _

/-- The composition of two pushforward functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
def pushforwardComp :
    pushforward f ⋙ pushforward g ≅ pushforward (f ≫ g) :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _

@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_inv_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl

/-- The composition of two pullback functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
identify to the pullback for the composition. -/
def pullbackComp :
    pullback g ⋙ pullback f ≅ pullback (f ≫ g) :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackComp _ _

/-- Pushforwards along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def pushforwardCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pushforward f ≅ pushforward g :=
    pushforwardNatIso _ (Opens.mapIso _ _ (hf ▸ rfl)) ≪≫
      SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforwardCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforwardCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl

/-- Inverse images along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def pullbackCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pullback f ≅ pullback g :=
  eqToIso (hf ▸ rfl)

lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv :
    conjugateEquiv ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).comp (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f))
      (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ g)) (pullbackComp f g).inv =
    (pushforwardComp f g).hom :=
  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv _ _

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc]
lemma pseudofunctor_associativity :
    (pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)).inv ≫
      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackComp g h).inv _ ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫
        Functor.whiskerLeft _ (pullbackComp f g).hom ≫ (pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).hom =
    eqToHom (by simp) := by
  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)
  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackComp g h) (pullback f)
  let e₃ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback h) (pullbackComp f g)
  let e₄ := pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h
  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.inv ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫ e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = _
  have : e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = (Functor.associator _ _ _).inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₁.hom :=
    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_assoc.{u}
      h.toRingCatSheafHom g.toRingCatSheafHom f.toRingCatSheafHom)
  simp [this]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc]
lemma pseudofunctor_left_unitality :
    (pullbackComp f (𝟙 Y)).inv ≫
      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackId Y).hom (pullback f) ≫ (Functor.leftUnitor _).hom =
        eqToHom (by simp) := by
  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (𝟙 _)
  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackId Y) (pullback f)
  let e₃ := (pullback f).leftUnitor
  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_id_comp.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)
  simp [← this]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc]
lemma pseudofunctor_right_unitality :
    (pullbackComp (𝟙 X) f).inv ≫
      Functor.whiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId X).hom ≫ (Functor.rightUnitor _).hom =
        eqToHom (by simp) := by
  let e₁ := pullbackComp (𝟙 _) f
  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId _)
  let e₃ := (pullback f).rightUnitor
  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_comp_id.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)
  simp [← this]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
attribute [local simp] pseudofunctor_associativity pseudofunctor_left_unitality
  pseudofunctor_right_unitality Bicategory.toNatTrans_conjugateEquiv
  conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom Adjunction.ofCat_comp conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv in
/-- The pseudofunctor from `Schemeᵒᵖ` to the bicategory of adjunctions which sends
a scheme `X` to the category `X.Modules` of sheaves of modules over `X`.
(This contains both the covariant and the contravariant functorialities of
these categories.) -/
@[simps! obj_obj map_l map_r map_adj
  mapId_hom_τl mapId_hom_τr mapId_inv_τl mapId_inv_τr
  mapComp_hom_τl mapComp_hom_τr mapComp_inv_τl mapComp_inv_τr]
def pseudofunctor :
    Pseudofunctor (LocallyDiscrete Scheme.{u}ᵒᵖ) (Adj Cat) :=
  LocallyDiscrete.mkPseudofunctor
    (fun X ↦ Adj.mk (Cat.of X.unop.Modules))
    (fun f ↦ .mk (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f.unop).toCat)
    (fun _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackId _))
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardId _).symm))
    (fun _ _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackComp _ _).symm)
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardComp _ _)))

end Functorial

noncomputable section Restriction

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g]

/-- Restriction of an `𝒪ₓ`-module along an open immersion.
This is isomorphic to the pullback functor (see `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`)
but has better defeqs. -/
def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
  SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
    ⟨Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
abbrev restrict (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : X.Modules :=
  (restrictFunctor f).obj M

@[simp] lemma restrict_obj (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] (U) :
    Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp] lemma restrict_map (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] {U V} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _) ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents
      (fun U ↦ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ↦ rfl) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ⊣ pushforward f := by
  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_
  · ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x)
  · ext U x
    have : (f.appIso U.unop).inv ≫ f.app _ ≫
      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by
      rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← X.presheaf.map_id]; rfl
    exact congr($this x)

instance : IsIso (restrictAdjunction f).counit :=
  inferInstanceAs (IsIso <| (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso f).hom)

instance : (restrictFunctor f).IsLeftAdjoint := (restrictAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Full := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.full
instance : (pushforward f).Faithful := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.faithful

@[simp]

527	has supremum `⊤`.  Project-local: translates the categorical `J.CoversTop` condition carried by a

529	consumes. -/
530	private lemma coversTop_iSup_eq_top {T : Type*} [TopologicalSpace T] {I : Type*}
531	    (Y : I → TopologicalSpace.Opens T)
532	    (hY : (Opens.grothendieckTopology T).CoversTop Y) :
533	    ⨆ i, Y i = ⊤ := by
534	  rw [eq_top_iff]
535	  intro x _
536	  rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
537	  obtain ⟨U, f, hf, hU⟩ := hY ⊤ x (TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_top x)
538	  obtain ⟨i, ⟨g⟩⟩ := hf
539	  exact ⟨i, (leOfHom g) hU⟩

541	/-- **Finite presentation cover from quasi-coherence (Stacks 01I8, Route B step B1).**  For a
542	quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`, there is a quasi-coherence datum `q` (a cover `q.X` of
543	`Spec R` together with a presentation of `F.over (q.X i)` for each `i`) and a *finite* standard
544	refinement of it: finitely many `g : Fin n → R` with `span (range g) = ⊤` and indices
545	`φ : Fin n → q.I` such that each basic open `D(g j)` lies inside the cover member `q.X (φ j)`.  The
546	presentation of `F.over (q.X (φ j))` carried by `q` is what steps B2–B4 restrict to `D(g j)`.
547	Project-local: packages Mathlib's `QuasicoherentData` (local generation) with the affine finite
548	basic-open refinement (`exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover`) in the exact form the Route B keystone
549	descent consumes. -/
550	lemma qcoh_finite_presentation_cover (F : (Spec R).Modules)
551	    [hF : F.IsQuasicoherent] :
552	    ∃ (q : SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData.{u, u, u, u} F)
553	      (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (φ : Fin n → q.I),
554	      (∀ j, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g j) ≤ q.X (φ j)) ∧ Ideal.span (Set.range g) = ⊤ := by
555	  obtain ⟨q⟩ := hF.nonempty_quasicoherentData
556	  have htop : ⨆ i, q.X i = ⊤ := coversTop_iSup_eq_top q.X q.coversTop
557	  obtain ⟨n, g, φ, hgU, hspan⟩ := exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover q.X htop
558	  exact ⟨q, n, g, φ, hgU, hspan⟩

=== iSup→cover for opens ===
namespace presieveOfCovering

variable {ι : Type v} (U : ι → Opens X)

/-- The sieve generated by `presieveOfCovering U` is a member of the Grothendieck topology.
-/
theorem mem_grothendieckTopology :
    Sieve.generate (presieveOfCovering U) ∈ Opens.grothendieckTopology X (iSup U) := by
  intro x hx
  obtain ⟨i, hxi⟩ := Opens.mem_iSup.mp hx
  exact ⟨U i, Opens.leSupr U i, ⟨U i, 𝟙 _, Opens.leSupr U i, ⟨i, rfl⟩, Category.id_comp _⟩, hxi⟩

/-- An index `i : ι` can be turned into a dependent pair `(V, f)`, where `V` is an open set and
`f : V ⟶ iSup U` is a member of `presieveOfCovering U f`.
-/
def homOfIndex (i : ι) : Σ V, { f : V ⟶ iSup U // presieveOfCovering U f } :=
  ⟨U i, Opens.leSupr U i, i, rfl⟩

/-- By using the axiom of choice, a dependent pair `(V, f)` where `f : V ⟶ iSup U` is a member of
`presieveOfCovering U f` can be turned into an index `i : ι`, such that `V = U i`.
-/
def indexOfHom (f : Σ V, { f : V ⟶ iSup U // presieveOfCovering U f }) : ι :=
  f.2.2.choose

theorem indexOfHom_spec (f : Σ V, { f : V ⟶ iSup U // presieveOfCovering U f }) :
    f.1 = U (indexOfHom U f) :=
  f.2.2.choose_spec

end presieveOfCovering

end TopCat.Presheaf
